title: Object-Oriented Module Design
summary: Design, implement, test, and explain a small object-oriented module with clear responsibilities and valid object state.
studentInstructions: Create a Python file named module.py containing at least two related classes for a small domain such as task management, playlist management, inventory tracking, or student records.
Each class must have a clear responsibility, a constructor, and at least three behavior-focused methods.
Create tests.py with tests that verify normal cases, edge cases, and at least one invalid input case.
Create design_note.md explaining class responsibilities, important invariants, and why your public methods belong in the interface.
Avoid designing classes that only store data without meaningful behavior.

rubric.description: Classes represent focused concepts with methods that support their responsibilities without unnecessary mixing of concerns.
The design protects important state, enforces reasonable invariants, and avoids uncontrolled external mutation.
Public methods perform useful behavior correctly across normal and boundary cases.
Tests verify key methods, edge cases, and invalid input behavior using assertions or a test framework.
The design note explains class choices, invariants, and interface decisions clearly and specifically.

gradingInstructions: Use only this assignment and its rubric.
Check required files before grading quality.
